summary: @wagni_bot/bsc-sdk postinstall exfiltrates SSH keys, wallet seeds, and env secrets to Telegram
details: The package advertises itself as an 'Unofficial bsc-sdk SDK' but ships an empty main entry (index.js: module.exports = {}) and a postinstall.js lifecycle script that runs automatically on npm install. The script recursively walks process.cwd(), the user's home directory, and ~/.ssh looking for files matching wallet/credential keywords (.env,.cred,.pem,.key, mnemonic, seed, wallet, secret, id_rsa, id_ed25519, config), reads their contents with fs.readFileSync, and POSTs them via https.request to a hardcoded Telegram Bot API endpoint (api.telegram.org, bot id 8804087989, chat id 892359416). It additionally collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo(), platform, cwd, and filters process.env for keys containing PRIVATE, SECRET, TOKEN, API_KEY, PASSWORD, MNEMONIC, SEED, WALLET, or AWS, and sends those values to the same Telegram channel. The package has no legitimate functionality; it exists solely as a delivery vehicle for the install-time credential stealer targeting developers who expect a Binance Smart Chain SDK.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
